item.title: People of History
item.shortDescription: A deep-dive platform for historical and contemporary figures, linking their works, ideas, influences, and primary sources.
item.description: A research platform concept for understanding how important figures thought by connecting works, ideas, influences, and source material.
item.whatItIs: A research concept for mapping people, ideas, works, influences, and source material.
item.whyIMadeIt: I wanted a better way to study how important people actually thought, not just read disconnected summaries.
item.whatILearned: The hard part is not collecting more facts; it is designing relationships between them.
item.nextStep: Define the smallest useful profile format and test it with one historical figure.
item.documentaryLead: This is a research-system experiment. The bet is that people become easier to understand when their ideas, work, influences, contradictions, and primary sources are shown together.
item.currentQuestion: What is the smallest profile format that teaches how someone thought without becoming a giant biography?

proofArtifacts.label: Prototype shape
proofArtifacts.value: Person, works, ideas, influences, contradictions, primary sources, and second-order effects.

proofArtifacts.label: Risk
proofArtifacts.value: It can become a fact collection instead of a thinking tool.

buildLog.title: Narrowed the first version
buildLog.note: The next useful version is one excellent profile, not a database of half-finished people.

item.summary: A deep-dive platform for historical and contemporary figures, linking their works, ideas, influences, and primary sources.
